program.name: ANZ Graduate Programs
program.duration_text: You’ll get 18 or 24 months of critical experiences across our business, including exposure to a diverse range of people, environments and situations.

eligibility.work_rights: When you apply, you should be an Australian or New Zealand citizen, or hold permanent residency in the program country you are applying for.
eligibility.graduation_window: When you apply, you should be in your final year of university studies (bachelor degree or higher), or have graduated in the last three years.
minimum_requirements: Be an Australian or New Zealand citizen, or hold permanent residency in the program country you are applying for.
Be in your final year of university studies (bachelor degree or higher), or have graduated in the last three years.

stages.name: Digital interview and game-based assessment
stages.details: A set of games that help ANZ get to know you better.

stages.name: Personality questionnaire
stages.details: Questions about how you like to work.

stages.name: Interview preparation
stages.details: Tips from past grads on how to do well in the interview.

stages.name: Face to face interview
stages.details: Meet some leaders so they can learn more about you, and you can get to know ANZ.

tips: Use the interview preparation stage to get tips from past grads on how to do well in the interview.

exec_themes: Cost discipline and productivity (tight control of expenses while still investing in the business).
Risk management and stronger non-financial risk controls (including conduct, complaints, fraud/scams and financial crime focus).
Capital strength and efficiency (maintaining a robust balance sheet and improving capital efficiency).
Diversified earnings across Banking and Markets (using multiple business lines to support revenue and profit).
Integration and value capture from the Suncorp Bank acquisition (including a larger earnings base from a full-year contribution).
Support customers through the climate transition (building capability to assess climate/nature risk and help customers transition).
commercial_context.profit_engine: ANZ primarily makes money from a diversified mix of: (1) Banking earnings (net interest income plus fees) supported by balance sheet growth and improved risk-adjusted margins, and (2) Markets income (customer activity-driven, with repeated >$1b first-half income in recent years mentioned), with results amplified by cost discipline, capital management, and the added earnings contribution from Suncorp Bank.
commercial_context.headwinds: Management cited macro headwinds including cash rate reductions, higher funding costs, and seasonal impacts (noted as around $200 million of headwinds in 1H25).
commercial_context.esg: ANZ’s ESG approach is organised around seven “most material” issues (including environmental sustainability, ethics/culture, financial wellbeing, housing, information security, responsible customer engagement, and improving non-financial risk management). Key climate elements include a Climate & Environment Strategy aimed at “financing a sustainable transition,” ambitions to transition its lending portfolio to net-zero financed emissions by 2050, and a target to fund and facilitate at least $100b in social and environmental activities by September 2030. ANZ also states it achieved an earlier operational emissions goal and reset to an 85% Scope 1 & 2 reduction by 2030 against a 2024 baseline (commencing 1 Oct 2025).
commercial_context.recent_pivot: A recent emphasis highlighted in results communications is the framing of ANZ around two main businesses—Banking and Markets—and the step-change in earnings and balance sheet following the first full half/full year contribution from Suncorp Bank. (A broader strategic “pivot” beyond this framing/integration is not explicitly stated in the accessed sources.)

notes: The page lists streams for the Australian Graduate Program and typical streams for the New Zealand Graduate Program.
The page describes program duration as 18 or 24 months and mentions six-monthly rotations.
The page mentions graduates in India and locates the Group Capability Centre in Bengaluru.
Eligibility requirements are listed under the 'Am I eligible?' section.
The recruitment steps are listed under the 'New Zealand and Australia process' section.

Profit engine and headwinds are drawn mainly from the 2025 Half Year Results CFO speaking notes/transcript excerpts; they describe drivers and pressures but do not fully define ANZ’s business model in one place.
ESG content is well-supported by ANZ’s ESG webpages and the 2025 ESG Report, but this summary simplifies a larger reporting suite.
Recent pivot is interpreted as a shift in how ANZ presents/organises performance (Banking vs Markets) plus the impact of Suncorp Bank; the sources do not label it as a formal strategic pivot.
